factors.summary: Overall demand is showing mixed trends with growth in certain segments but declines in others.

evidence.text: Our worldwide operations are affected by global and regional industrial, economic and political factors, trade policies and trends. They are also affected by changes in the general level of economic activity, such as changes in business and consumer spending, construction and shipping activity as well as short-term economic factors such as currency fluctuations, commodity price volatility and supply disruptions.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices to mitigate supply chain and inflationary pressures.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments due to strong demand in certain regions and product lines.

evidence.text: Organic sales for the three months ended March 31, 2025 increased by 2% compared with the same period of 2024. The organic increase was primarily due to our Climate Solutions Americas segment as strong end-market demand continued to drive higher volumes. In addition, higher volumes benefited our Climate Solutions Transportation segment.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, particularly in the Climate Solutions Americas segment.

evidence.text: Organic sales for the three months ended March 31, 2025 increased by 2% compared with the same period of 2024. The organic increase was primarily due to our Climate Solutions Americas segment as strong end-market demand continued to drive higher volumes. In addition, higher volumes benefited our Climate Solutions Transportation segment.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing in certain end-markets, particularly in Climate Solutions Americas and Transportation, but declining in others like Climate Solutions Europe.

evidence.text: For the three months ended March 31, 2025, Net sales were $2.6 billion, a 9% increase compared with the same period of 2024. The components of the year-over-year change were as follows: Net sales Organic 9% Foreign currency translation —% Total % change in Net sales 9% The organic increase in Net sales of 9% was driven by continued strong results in the segment. Growth in our residential business (up 20%) was primarily driven by strong end-market demand and pricing improvements compared to the prior year. In addition, ongoing customer demand in our commercial business (up 15%) further benefited segment results. These amounts were partially offset by reduced end-market demand in our light commercial business (down 34%).

factors.summary: End markets are showing varying momentum, with strong demand in Climate Solutions Americas and Transportation, but slower demand in Europe and Asia Pacific.

evidence.text: For the three months ended March 31, 2025, Net sales were $2.6 billion, a 9% increase compared with the same period of 2024. The components of the year-over-year change were as follows: Net sales Organic 9% Foreign currency translation —% Total % change in Net sales 9% The organic increase in Net sales of 9% was driven by continued strong results in the segment. Growth in our residential business (up 20%) was primarily driven by strong end-market demand and pricing improvements compared to the prior year. In addition, ongoing customer demand in our commercial business (up 15%) further benefited segment results. These amounts were partially offset by reduced end-market demand in our light commercial business (down 34%).

factors.summary: Input costs are stable as the company is able to offset them through pricing and productivity improvements.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased by $60 million compared with the three months ended March 31, 2024. As a result, gross margin as a percentage of Net sales increased by 210 basis points compared with the same period of 2024. The prior period included inventory step-up and backlog amortization resulting from the recognition of acquired assets of the VCS Business at fair value which are now fully amortized. These costs had a 200 basis point unfavorable impact on the prior period gross margin as a percentage of Net sales. In addition, ongoing customer demand, pricing improvements and our continued focus on productivity initiatives further benefited gross margin.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with some increase in compensation and employee-related costs.

evidence.text: For the three months ended March 31, 2025, Selling, general and administrative expenses were $729 million, a 10% decrease compared with the same period of 2024. The decrease relates to productivity initiatives associated with our portfolio transformation and synergies associated with the integration of the VCS Business. These benefits were partially offset by higher compensation and other employee-related costs.

factors.summary: Market share is stable or improving, with the company well-positioned to benefit from favorable secular trends.

evidence.text: We believe our business segments are well positioned to benefit from favorable secular trends, including the mega-trends of urbanization, population growth and demographic shifts, food security and safety, digitalization, global connectivity and energy efficiency. Coupled with our industry-leading brands and track record of innovation, we continue to provide market-leading solutions for our customers.

factors.summary: Management is confident in their strategies, as evidenced by their actions to mitigate risks and invest in growth areas.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include geopolitical factors, economic conditions, and currency fluctuations, which management is actively monitoring.

evidence.text: We are actively monitoring evolving macroeconomic conditions and recent trade policy announcements. Based on our preliminary analysis, we expect to fully mitigate our expected 2025 impact from tariffs announced to date through supply chain and productivity actions as well as approximately $300 million of incremental product pricing actions. To date, tariffs have not had a material impact on our business and we are deploying additional strategies, including cost containment measures, to limit future exposure in this current market environment.

factors.summary: The company has a clear long-term strategy focused on becoming a pure-play climate and energy solutions provider through portfolio transformation and strategic investments.

evidence.text: During 2024, we completed several activities designed to simplify our business portfolio, transforming it into a pure-play climate and energy solutions provider. On January 2, 2024, we acquired the climate solutions business (the 'VCS Business') of Viessmann Group GmbH & Co. KG ('Viessmann').

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include economic conditions, currency fluctuations, and geopolitical risks, which management is actively monitoring.

evidence.text: Our worldwide operations are affected by global and regional industrial, economic and political factors, trade policies and trends. factors.summary: The integration of the VCS Business and new product launches are catalysts for growth.

evidence.text: During 2024, we completed several activities designed to simplify our business portfolio, transforming it into a pure-play climate and energy solutions provider. On January 2, 2024, we acquired the climate solutions business (the 'VCS Business') of Viessmann Group GmbH & Co. KG ('Viessmann'). The VCS Business, primarily reported in the Climate Solutions Europe segment, is a premier residential and light commercial HVAC provider in Europe that expanded our portfolio to offer a global, comprehensive suite of sustainable and innovative building and cold-chain solutions.

factors.summary: R&D spending is increasing, with investments in new product development and digital controls technologies.

evidence.text: Research and development costs relate to new product development and new technology innovation. Due to the variable nature of program development schedules, year-over-year spending levels can fluctuate. In addition, we continue to invest to prepare for future product innovations and digital controls technologies.

factors.summary: The company is adopting digital technologies and automation, which are contributing to productivity and competitive advantages.

evidence.text: Our portfolio includes industry-leading brands such as Carrier, Viessmann, Toshiba, Automated Logic and Carrier Transicold that offer innovative heating, ventilating, air conditioning ('HVAC') and cold chain transportation solutions to enhance the lives we live and the world we share. We also provide a broad array of related building services, including audit, design, installation, system integration, repair, maintenance and monitoring.

factors.summary: New products are gaining traction, particularly in the Climate Solutions Transportation segment with improved end-market demand and pricing.

evidence.text: For the three months ended March 31, 2025, Net sales were $651 million, a 26% decrease compared to the same period of 2024. The components of the year-over-year change were as follows: Net sales Organic 2% Foreign currency translation (1)% Acquisitions and divestitures, net (27)% Total % change in Net sales (26)% The organic increase in Net sales of 2% was primarily driven by volume growth within certain end-markets compared with the prior year. Container results increased (up 20%) due to improved end-market demand and pricing improvements. These results were partially offset by our transport refrigeration business (down 3%) as lower end-market demand in Europe and North America more than offset improved end-market demand in Asia.

factors.summary: Interest rate changes are affecting the company's financials, with a focus on managing debt and interest costs.

evidence.text: Consistent with our capital allocation strategy, we reduced our outstanding debt by approximately $3 billion over the course of 2024 and repaid an additional $1.2 billion during the current period.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are impacting net sales and costs, with some unfavorable effects in certain regions.

evidence.text: For the three months ended March 31, 2025, Net sales were $1.2 billion, a 10% decrease compared with the same period of 2024. The components of the year-over-year change were as follows: Net sales Organic (7)% Foreign currency translation (3)% Total % change in Net sales (10)% The organic decrease in Net sales of 7% was driven by ongoing challenges in certain end-markets compared with the prior year. Results in our residential and light commercial business decreased (down 11%) due to lower volumes across the region as economic conditions, inflationary cost pressures and regulatory uncertainty impacted end-market demand. The reduction was partially offset by continued growth in our commercial business (up 5%) as a result of strong end-market demand and pricing improvements.

evidence.text: We are actively monitoring evolving macroeconomic conditions and recent trade policy announcements. Based on our preliminary analysis, we expect to fully mitigate our expected 2025 impact from tariffs announced to date through supply chain and productivity actions as well as approximately $300 million of incremental product pricing actions. To date, tariffs have not had a material impact on our business and we are deploying additional strategies, including cost containment measures, to limit future exposure in this current market environment.

factors.summary: The company is showing improvement in debt leverage, with net debt decreasing and a reduction in outstanding debt. These actions indicate a positive trend in managing financial obligations.

evidence.text: As of March 31, 2025, we had cash and cash equivalents of $1.7 billion... Net debt to net capitalization 40 % 37 %
Consistent with our capital allocation strategy, we reduced our outstanding debt by approximately $3 billion over the course of 2024 and repaid an additional $1.2 billion during the current period.

factors.summary: The company has been engaging in significant merger and acquisition (M&A) activity, including both divestitures and acquisitions, as part of a strategic portfolio transformation. This activity reflects a focus on simplifying the business and positioning itself as a pure-play climate and energy solutions provider.

evidence.text: On June 2, 2024, we completed the divestiture of our Access Solutions business for cash proceeds of $5.0 billion... proceeds were used to fund repayment of debt, invest in organic and inorganic growth initiatives, capital returns to shareowners as well as for general corporate purposes.

During 2024, we completed several activities designed to simplify our business portfolio, transforming it into a pure-play climate and energy solutions provider. On January 2, 2024, we acquired the climate solutions business (the 'VCS Business') of Viessmann Group GmbH & Co. KG ('Viessmann').

factors.summary: The company is focused on enhancing shareholder returns through its share repurchase program and potential use of excess cash for buybacks or dividends. However, the evidence highlights the impact of macroeconomic conditions and trade policies, which may influence the company's ability to execute these plans fully. The company has $1.9 billion remaining under its share repurchase authorization as of March 31, 2025, and is taking measures to mitigate risks such as tariffs through supply chain adjustments and pricing actions.

evidence.text: Since the initial authorization in February 2021, our Board of Directors authorized the repurchase of up to $7.1 billion of our outstanding common stock... $1.9 billion remaining under the current authorization at March 31, 2025.

We are actively monitoring evolving macroeconomic conditions and recent trade policy announcements. Based on our preliminary analysis, we expect to fully mitigate our expected 2025 impact from tariffs announced to date through supply chain and productivity actions as well as approximately $300 million of incremental product pricing actions. To date, tariffs have not had a material impact on our business and we are deploying additional strategies, including cost containment measures, to limit future exposure in this current market environment.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving compared to the prior period, driven by higher gross margins and effective cost management. The increase in gross margin, both in absolute terms and as a percentage of net sales, has significantly contributed to this improvement. Additionally, higher accounts payable balances have helped offset increases in accounts receivable and inventory, further supporting the positive cash flow trend.

The prior period included inventory step-up and backlog amortization resulting from the recognition of acquired assets of the VCS Business at fair value which are now fully amortized. These costs had a 200 basis point unfavorable impact on the prior period gross margin as a percentage of Net sales. In addition, ongoing customer demand, pricing improvements and our continued focus on productivity initiatives further benefited gross margin.
